event: Surrealist encounter between a grotesque figure and a human
symbols: cross held by the figure — religious authority twisted into something grotesque
towering figure — an overwhelming, distorted power
smaller human form — vulnerability before incomprehensible forces
description: A pencil or graphite drawing on sketchbook paper showing a tall, elongated figure with a grotesque, bird-like or equine head wearing dark robes and holding what appears to be a cross or scepter. The figure towers over a much smaller nude or semi-nude human figure below. The drawing uses heavy crosshatching for shadows and has a dreamlike, surrealist quality. The lines are precise but the subject matter is fantastical and unsettling.

_meta.title: Do You Know My Aunt Eliza?
